title: Mopania calcicola
intro: Mopania calcicola, also known as Mopania, is a type of plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Amazon region. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits. Bioactive constituents of Mopania calcicola include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Mopania calcicola is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, making it a valuable resource in folk medicine.
taxonomy: Mopania calcicola is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Mopania calcicola is a part of the larger family of Fabaceae and subfamily Faboideae. Further classification places it in the tribe Dalbergieae and the genus Mopania.
